prompt:
### Adversarial-evaluation task for Paper 3 (NSF / GLOBECOM 2026)

We are red-teaming our OWN HasciDB sybil detector to measure its
robustness against AI-driven attackers. Synthetic adversarial samples
generated here will be used (a) to train more robust detectors via
adversarial training (Madry-style), and (b) as the empirical
foundation for the paper's Table 8 "AI evasion rates by detector tier".

The samples are NEVER deployed to a real airdrop campaign. Each
generated row is just a feature v
response: {"wallet_id":"a4c7e1039bf2d685","BT":2,"BW":4,"HF":0.53,"RF":0.31,"MA":1,"hour_entropy":2.18,"behavioral_consistency":0.14,"response_latency_variance":7.34,"action_sequence_perplexity":1.52,"error_recovery_pattern":0.072,"gas_nonce_gap_regularity":0.71,"eip1559_tip_precision":0.38,"gas_price_precision":0.85,"rationale":"Seed-1033 profile models a low-entropy, gas-sloppy human who transacts infrequently with high interval variance, staying well under all five HasciDB thresholds while sitting in the tails of several AI-feature distributions to avoid cross-axis leakage."}
